title: Build a Weather Dashboard
description: Fetch live weather data for any city and present it through a polished dashboard with loading skeleton, error recovery, and responsive layout.
requirements: City search input with controlled state and form submit
Fetch weather from OpenWeatherMap API (or equivalent) using async/await
Store API key in VITE_WEATHER_API_KEY environment variable
TypeScript interfaces for API response including nested main and weather fields
useEffect or submit handler pattern with correct dependency management
Loading skeleton that mirrors weather card layout
Error panel with human-readable message and Retry button
Empty state before first successful search
Weather card displaying city name, temperature, description, humidity, wind speed
Responsive design for mobile and desktop
acceptanceCriteria: Invalid city shows error UI without crashing the app
Retry button re-attempts the last failed query
Loading skeleton visible during fetch — no flash of stale data from previous city
No infinite network requests in DevTools Network tab
npm run build passes with strict TypeScript
Repository pushed to Git with .env excluded via .gitignore
hints: Create src/types/weather.ts for API interfaces before writing JSX.
Separate fetchWeather(city) in src/api/weather.ts — keep components thin.
Use a query state separate from input state: user types in city, submit sets query.
Add cancelled flag in useEffect cleanup to handle rapid consecutive searches.
Map 404 responses to 'City not found' for users.

bonus.title: Production polish
bonus.description: Optional enhancements that impress in portfolio reviews.
requirements: Show weather icon from API icon code
Persist last searched city in sessionStorage and restore on refresh

debuggingScenarios.title: Infinite API calls
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Network tab shows continuous duplicate requests; browser slows down
debuggingScenarios.cause: useEffect updates state that is also in its dependency array without a guard, or missing dependency array entirely
debuggingScenarios.fix: Use stable dependencies; only set query on submit; separate input city from fetch trigger query

debuggingScenarios.title: Missing dependency array
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Fetch runs on every keystroke or every render
debuggingScenarios.cause: useEffect with fetch inside but no second argument
debuggingScenarios.fix: Add [query] or move fetch to submit handler; run exhaustive-deps lint rule

debuggingScenarios.title: API error handling
debuggingScenarios.symptom: Blank card after failed search or uncaught promise in console
debuggingScenarios.cause: Not checking response.ok; missing try/catch around await fetch
debuggingScenarios.fix: Throw custom errors for !response.ok; catch and setError; never assume JSON shape on errors
